name: Cappellaci Di Zucca
recipeIngredient: 1 Recipe basic pasta dough, rolled out to thinnest setting on pasta machine
recipeInstructions:
1. Bake the squash for 1 hour at 350 degrees F (°C). Scoop it out, removing seeds and skin.
2. Bring 6 quarts water to boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
3. In a large mixing bowl, mix the squash, eggs, cheese, and nutmeg together until smooth and homogenous. Season with salt and pepper; set aside.
4. Cut pasta sheets into 3-inch squares. Place 1 teaspoon of squash filling in the center of each square.
5. Fold to form triangles, pressing firmly around all edges. Take two further corners and bring them together, pressing firmly to attach and form little hats.
6. Place cappellacci in boiling water and lower the heat to a high simmer.
7. Cook until tender, about 2 to 3 minutes.
8. Melt butter in a 12 to 14 inch saute pan and add sage. Carefully drain pasta and place it in the pan with butter.
9. Warm gently for 1 minute and serve. Sprinkle with remaining cheese.
Yield: 4 as antipasto
